danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite variety; organic vs. mechanical creature designs
danger_signals.meaning: World state corruption: the Chaos Engine's influence manifests as grotesque hybrid creatures

danger_signals.visual_form: Anachronistic creature appearances in steampunk Victorian setting
danger_signals.meaning: Temporal chaos: the Chaos Engine breaks chronological boundaries, mixing time periods as visible threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental decay across four worlds; terrain corruption visual progression
danger_signals.meaning: Expanding Chaos Engine influence zone; severity increases with proximity to source

danger_signals.visual_form: Interactive environmental markers requiring weapon fire/special attacks to reveal
danger_signals.meaning: Chaos Engine's control points; player must visibly reclaim territory by disabling nodes

safety_signals.signal: Upgrade stations (end of every second level)
safety_signals.visual_form: Designated safe zone with merchant/vendor interface
safety_signals.meaning: Sanctuary from Chaos; progression checkpoints where players fortify themselves with currency
Resource abundance = safer zones; scarcity = danger proximity

safety_signals.visual_form: Level exit markers requiring node activation chain completion
safety_signals.meaning: Progression to next cleared zone; visual manifestation of player agency reclaiming space

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character costume/class archetypes (Navvie, Thug, Brigand, Mercenary, Gentleman, Preacher/Scientist)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Nodes activate via player weapon fire; territory control through activation sequences

faction_indicators.faction: The Chaos Engine and Baron Fortesque
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mechanical/organic hybrid aesthetic; future technology retro-engineered into Victorian steampunk
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Monster and automata enemy types; creature design indicates assimilation and corruption
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Vile terrain, node placements, monster spawns; visual evidence of territory control and experimentation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Steampunk Victorian architecture and aesthetics (implicit in world design)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Quarantined Britain setting; isolated island state; cut communication lines (narrative context)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Implied through character archetypes and setting; not directly shown but referenced in narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Royal Family, Parliament, refugees escaped; normal order maintained before Engine activation

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active Chaos corruption spreading across four landscapes
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progression through four distinct worlds showing increasing environmental degradation; monster/automata density increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively patrol and attack; nodes must be disabled to reclaim space; player movement grants territory control

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Chaos Engine fully sentient and dominant
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final level descends into 'cellars in the hall of machines'; pure mechanical/technological aesthetic dominates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Baron Fortesque revealed as trapped narrator; direct confrontation with the corrupting force

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Telegram wires cut; no ships enter ports; visual barrier between Britain and mainland Europe (implied through mission briefing)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mercenaries infiltrate as only viable response; world sealed off requiring external intervention

environmental_storytelling.example: Contrast between intended industrial progress (retro-engineered future tech) and actual chaos (corrupted landscape) communicates thematic conflict

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type diversity as chronological corruption
environmental_storytelling.example: Prehistoric beasts alongside mechanical automata signals time itself is fractured by the Chaos Engine
Moving through four worlds with increasing Chaos manifestation; final descent into machine hall represents approaching source of corruption

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character archetype selection as faction communication
environmental_storytelling.example: Six character classes with distinct weapons/abilities represent diverse mercenary recruiting pool unified against common threat

environmental_storytelling.technique: Node activation mechanics as player agency over world-state
environmental_storytelling.example: Firing weapons at environmental nodes to open doors manifests player force literally reclaiming Chaos-corrupted territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hostile patrols; aggressive spawning; block progression until nodes activated; visual manifestation of corruption spread

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Temporal anomaly caused by Engine experimentation
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appear anachronistically in Victorian landscapes; signal world-state breakdown at fundamental level
AI partner mirrors player actions and observations; developed through play-testing observation of human behavior patterns
Trapped within machine; narrates game in post-assimilation state; final boss confrontation reveals narrator identity

notes: The Chaos Engine uses a steampunk alternate-timeline setting to create visual contrast between Victorian order and Chaos-induced corruption. World-state is communicated primarily through enemy/environment variety, progression through degrading landscapes, and the mechanical metaphor of node activation representing territorial reclamation. The game's visual design strategy relies on: (1) anachronism as danger signal (prehistoric creatures in industrial settings), (2) mechanical/organic hybridization as aesthetic of corruption, (3) level progression as narrative descent toward chaos source, (4) character class diversity as faction identity. No explicit color-coding or symbolic sigils documented in source, but environmental architecture and enemy design carry political/temporal meaning through thematic contrast.
